introSection: Great Chase was a steel kiddie roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
It opened on May 8, 1999 as part of the transformation of Adventure World into Six Flaga America.

It replaced the former The Great Alonzo's Cannonball Coaster, being located in the Looney Tunes Movie Town section of the park.

Although a ride of the same name exists at Six Flags New England, and previously Six Flags Elitch Gardens, the New England version is a E&F Miler Industries roller coaster, and the former Great Chase at Six Flags Elitch Gardens was an Allan Herschell Company Little Dipper model.

Great Chase closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride begins when the train is pulled up the 13 foot tall lift hill before dropping into a helix and turn. Because the track is so short, the coaster sends guests through 3 times.

fullText:
Great Chase was a steel kiddie roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA.

It opened on May 8, 1999 as part of the transformation of Adventure World into Six Flaga America.

It replaced the former The Great Alonzo's Cannonball Coaster, being located in the Looney Tunes Movie Town section of the park.

Although a ride of the same name exists at Six Flags New England, and previously Six Flags Elitch Gardens, the New England version is a E&F Miler Industries roller coaster, and the former Great Chase at Six Flags Elitch Gardens was an Allan Herschell Company Little Dipper model.

Great Chase closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

The ride begins when the train is pulled up the 13 foot tall lift hill before dropping into a helix and turn. Because the track is so short, the coaster sends guests through 3 times.

Blue track and supports.
